faq.question: How to blur background on iPhone?
faq.answer: To blur background on iPhone, download Make Blur, open your photo, and let the app auto-detect the background (or draw a custom mask). Then choose a blur effect—Bokeh, motion blur, gaussian blur, or another style—and save. You can also blur faces for privacy in the same app.

faq.question: What is the best app to blur background on iPhone?
faq.answer: Make Blur is a dedicated iPhone app to blur backgrounds and faces. It offers auto-detect background, face blur for privacy, seven blur types including Bokeh, motion blur, and gaussian blur, plus a user-friendly interface. Early users rate it 4.5 stars.

faq.question: How to blur photo on iPhone?
faq.answer: To blur photo on iPhone, use Make Blur. Open your photo, select the area to blur or use auto-detect for the background, blur faces if needed, then pick Bokeh, motion blur, gaussian blur, or another effect and save.

faq.question: Can I blur background of photo for free?
faq.answer: Yes. Make Blur is free to download and lets you blur backgrounds, blur faces for privacy, and apply seven effects including Bokeh, motion blur, and gaussian blur. Some advanced options may be optional in-app purchases; core editing works without paying.

faq.question: How to blur image on iPhone?
faq.answer: To blur image on iPhone, download Make Blur. Open your image, auto-detect the background or draw a mask, use face blur if you need privacy, then choose Bokeh, motion blur, gaussian blur, or another style and save.

faq.question: What blur effects can I use to blur background on iPhone?
faq.answer: When you blur background on iPhone with Make Blur, you can use motion blur, gaussian blur, ghosting, pixelate, hexagonal, crystalize, and Bokeh. Bokeh adds a shallow-depth-of-field look with soft out-of-focus highlights. All seven styles work for background or selected areas.

faq.question: Why choose Make Blur over other blur apps?
faq.answer: Make Blur stands out with auto-detect background, dedicated face blur for privacy, seven blur effects including Bokeh, and a freemium model that keeps core tools free. Many competitors lock basic blur behind subscriptions or aggressive ads, while Make Blur focuses on an easy blur background and face-blur workflow first.

faq.question: How does Make Blur compare to iPhone's built-in blur feature?
faq.answer: While iPhone's built-in blur works in Portrait mode, Make Blur lets you blur any photo from your gallery, offers seven effects including Bokeh (motion blur, gaussian blur, ghosting, pixelate, hexagonal, crystalize, Bokeh), includes face blur for privacy, and auto-detect background on any photo. Make Blur gives more control and creative options than built-in iPhone blur.

faq.question: Is Make Blur better than other blur apps?
faq.answer: Make Blur offers advantages including: a fair freemium model (no forced subscription), seven blur effect types including Bokeh (more than many competitors), face blur for privacy, and auto-detect background. With a 4.5-star rating from early users and 10,000+ downloads, it's designed for ease of use, while many competing apps push subscriptions or limit key features behind paywalls.

faq.question: How do I blur faces in a photo on iPhone?
faq.answer: Use Make Blur’s face blur workflow: open your photo, choose the face-blur option, and apply blur to detected or selected faces before saving. It is intended for privacy when sharing group photos or public shots where you want to obscure identities without editing on a desktop.

faq.question: What does the Bokeh filter do in Make Blur?
faq.answer: The Bokeh filter simulates a shallow depth of field: your subject stays sharp while the background gets a soft blur with natural-looking highlight orbs—similar to a fast portrait lens. It is one of seven blur styles alongside motion blur, gaussian blur, ghosting, pixelate, hexagonal, and crystalize.
